paper_title: Study on the use of HBP in gilthead seabream

methods.description: Procedure for formulating and preparing experimental diets for gilthead seabream, including control and diets containing varying levels of Honeybee Pollen (HBP) or Honeybee Pollen Supercritical Fluid Extract (HBP_SFE). The diets were designed to be isoenergetic and isoproteic and meet the species' nutrient requirements.

steps.description: Formulate diets to meet nutrient requirements of gilthead seabream [30,31].
steps.searchable_summary: formulate animal feed based on nutritional requirements
inputs: list of ingredients (soybean meal, fish meal, corn gluten meal, fish oil, gelatinized starch, wheat gluten, mineral mix, vitamins mix, HBP, HBP_SFE)

steps.description: Ensure the diets were isoenergetic (21.5 MJ/kg on average) and isoproteic (39.3% crude protein on average).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate and adjust diet composition to achieve target energy and protein levels
outputs: final diet formulations meeting energy and protein targets

steps.description: Choose the two pollen and extract inclusion levels based on previous research and in order to have the same total polyphenol content both in P5 and P10 diets and in E0.5 and E1 diets, according to the HBP extraction yield and the extract phenol content as determined by Messina et al. [8].
steps.searchable_summary: determine inclusion levels for HBP and HBP_SFE based on previous data and target polyphenol content

steps.description: Finely chop HBP, mix with 10 mL of water to create a paste, then mix in fish oil.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare HBP for incorporation into feed

steps.description: Dissolve HBP_SFE initially in 10 mL of fish oil.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare HBP_SFE for incorporation into feed

steps.description: Before the final mixing, all ingredients were ground through a 0.5 mm sieve.
steps.searchable_summary: grind dry feed ingredients to a specific particle size

steps.description: Water was added to the ground mixture.
steps.searchable_summary: add water to ground feed mixture

steps.description: Incorporate HBP/HBP_SFE mixtures into the main mixture.
steps.searchable_summary: mix prepared HBP/HBP_SFE components into the main feed mixture
inputs: HBP-water-fish oil mixture (for P5, P10 diets)
HBP_SFE-fish oil solution (for E0.5, E1 diets)

steps.description: The mixture was pelleted through a 3 mm dye, using a meat-grinder (Bosch mod. MFW68660, Stuttgart, Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: pellet the feed mixture using a meat grinder with a 3mm dye

steps.description: The diets were dried in a ventilated oven at a temperature of 40 • C for 24 h. This temperature was chosen as it is like the temperature maintained in the hive (34.6 • C on average) to preserve the pollen quality.
steps.searchable_summary: dry feed pellets in a ventilated oven at 40C for 24 hours

steps.description: The feeds were stored at 4 • C until use.
steps.searchable_summary: store dried feed pellets at 4C

methods.description: Procedure for housing and maintaining gilthead seabream specimens under controlled experimental conditions for an adaptation period and the subsequent experimental feeding period.

steps.description: Obtain gilthead seabream specimens (n = 90; 294.7 ± 12.8 g initial body weight), normally fed, from a local farm.
steps.searchable_summary: acquire experimental animals (gilthead seabream) from a source

steps.description: Randomly allocate fish to 15 fiberglass tanks with a capacity of 220 L (6 fish per tank, 3 tanks per diet).
steps.searchable_summary: randomly assign fish to experimental tanks
outputs: fish allocated to tanks (6 fish/tank, 3 tanks/diet)

steps.description: Keep the fish for 15 days under the experimental conditions, for adaptation to the new conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain fish in experimental tanks for an adaptation period

steps.description: Equip the tanks with a biological filter, mechanical sand filter and UV ray system for water sterilization.
steps.searchable_summary: set up tank filtration and sterilization systems
outputs: tanks with filtration and sterilization systems

steps.description: Maintain water quality parameters: temperature 22 ± 0.5 • C, salinity 33 ± 2.0 g/L, dissolved oxygen 6.5 ± 1.1 mg/L, pH 7.9 ± 0.5, total ammonia nitrogen < 0.3 mg/L, nitrite < 0.01 mg/L, and nitrate < 38 mg/L.
steps.searchable_summary: monitor and maintain specific water quality parameters in tanks

steps.description: Maintain a dark:light cycle of 12:12 h.
steps.searchable_summary: control tank lighting to maintain a 12:12 hour dark:light cycle

methods.description: Procedure for sampling gilthead seabream after the experimental feeding period, including fasting, anesthesia, dissection, and collection and initial preservation of liver and fillet samples.

steps.description: After 30 days, sample a total of 12 gilthead seabream per dietary treatment (336.2 ± 11.4 g average final body weight).
steps.searchable_summary: select fish for sampling from each experimental group
outputs: selected fish for sampling (12 per diet)

steps.description: Sampled fish were subjected to a 24-h fast.
steps.searchable_summary: fast selected fish for 24 hours

steps.description: Anesthetize fasted fish by MS-222 (tricaine methane sulfonate) (250 mg/L).
steps.searchable_summary: anesthetize fish using MS-222

steps.description: Dissect anesthetized fish in cold conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: dissect anesthetized fish under cold conditions

steps.description: Collect biopsies from the liver (approximately 300 mg).
steps.searchable_summary: collect liver tissue samples

steps.description: Immediately preserve liver biopsies under PUREzol Reagent (Bio-Rad, Hercules, CA, USA) at −80 • C.
steps.searchable_summary: preserve liver biopsies in PUREzol reagent and store at -80C

steps.description: Collect fillets (n = 120).
steps.searchable_summary: collect fish fillet samples

steps.description: Preserve and cover fillets by ice.
steps.searchable_summary: preserve fish fillets by covering with ice

steps.description: Send ice-preserved fillets, in polystyrene containers, to the Animal Science Section of the Department of Agriculture, Food, Environment and Forestry (DAGRI) of the University of Florence (Florence, Italy), for the analyses scheduled.
steps.searchable_summary: transport ice-preserved fillets to external laboratory for analysis
